extra.message: User-controlled 'username' query parameter is concatenated directly into a SQL query via f-string and executed by run_sql_query/cursor.execute(). This enables SQL injection (e.g. username=" OR 1=1 --).

User-supplied fields (user.name, user.username, user.address, user.email, user.contact) from the PUT /user body are interpolated into an SQL INSERT via f-string and executed without parameterization, allowing SQL injection.
User-controlled 'username' query parameter is concatenated directly into a DELETE SQL statement via f-string, enabling SQL injection and potential mass deletion of rows.
user.address from the request body is interpolated directly into a DELETE SQL statement via f-string without quoting or parameterization, enabling SQL injection.

The POST /find endpoint accepts arbitrary JSON from the request body and passes it directly as the Mongo-style query filter to users.find(query). An attacker can submit operator-based queries (e.g. {"username": {"$ne": null}}) to bypass authentication logic or enumerate users, i.e. NoSQL injection.

cwe: CWE-943: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements in Data Query Logic

extra.message: The POST /reset endpoint deletes the SQL database file and the NoSQL database directory and re-initializes them without any authentication or authorization check, allowing any unauthenticated attacker to wipe all application data.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: The DELETE /user endpoint allows anonymous deletion of any user by username or address without any authentication or authorization check (broken access control).
The PUT /user endpoint allows creation of arbitrary user accounts without any authentication or authorization check.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: Passwords are hashed using MD5, which is cryptographically broken and unsuitable for password storage. Use a slow, salted password hash such as bcrypt/argon2/scrypt.

cwe: CWE-327: Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm

extra.message: Seeded user passwords are hashed using unsalted MD5 (a broken hashing algorithm) before insertion into the database.

cwe: CWE-327: Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm

extra.message: GET /select returns full user rows including the password (MD5) hash field to any unauthenticated caller, exposing sensitive credential material.

cwe: CWE-200: Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor
